candidates.stmt:
Roger is the Deputy Leader of the Lib Dem Group on Birmingham City Council. He serves on the Housing and Neighbourhoods Scrutiny Committee and is one of the four Councillors, one from each party, on the Council who successfully proposed the Climate Emergency Motion. He now sits on the Taskforce that was set up as a result.

Roger’s priorities are:

1)            To secure greater investment in the NHS, especially to cut waiting times to see GPs, improve care for those with mental health problems

2)            To improve education and youth services, increasing investment in our schools and to provide better facilities for our young people.

3)            To tackle crime and anti-social behaviour by increasing the size of our Neighbourhood Police Teams and protecting their local bases.

Roger believes the UK’s best interests are served by remaining a member of the EU and argues that a no deal Brexit would be particularly damaging to Yardley.

The coming General Election will be a fight between Labour and the Lib Dems in Yardley. Both parties have won the seat twice in the last 4 General Elections. As a local resident with a strong history of representing the area on the Council Roger is well placed to continue the Lib Dems history of success in the seat.
Your Reform UK Candidate for Acocks Green.
Your Reform UK Candidate for Acocks Green.

Hi I'm eddie, as you can see I previously served as the local councillor for many years before being voted out at the last election by 14 votes. I've lived in the allens cross for over 40yrs, my family and friends all live on the ward.

Me and my wife started a local residents association over 30yrs ago. We managed to put on trips for the local kids and pensioners and those who couldn't afford holidays, we also provided Christmas hampers for the vaunrable. And also a local raffle. We also put on discos and party's to pull the community together.

We've always put into the ward never taken out and I have a proven track record for the ward, gaining investment, along with community surgery's and events to bring us all together.

Your issues are my issues as we live on the allens cross! I haven't just popped up at election time I've been around 40yrs. If you want someone who lives on the ward, and will be accessible 7 days a week, who loves where they live then vote for me I won't let you down. VOTE EDDIE FREEMAN 7TH MAY.

Ever since I can remember The Labour Party has been part of my life. Being brought up within a large family in one of the poorest parts of the city during the 1970s encouraged me to want to help and achieve more for the people that lived in my community. I remember at a very young age standing in front of the Town hall holding a placard proclaiming the Labour Party and the right for free school milk against the Thatcher Government

I have worked in care since 1993 starting at the bottom and working my way up to management level. As soon as I started work I joined a trade union and became a Union Rep helping highlight to the other workers the finest origins of the Labour Party history, the labour movement. Today I am a NVQ assessor/trainer practicing what I preach.

I have campaigned over many years for Labour within different constituencies in the West Midlands; you may have met me during the Kate Booth campaign where I helped endlessly and most recently in the General Election for Preet Gill (What a result!).

My aims are to focus on the Tory cuts that are effecting all that live in Bartley green, which have been very evident when I have spoken to many members while on the doors steps this year. My mission will be to help raise awareness of the school cuts affecting all our children. The policing in the area being stretched due to the cuts also means crime is on the rise. I also want to save local services from closure.

I will ensure that the Labour movement in Bartley will be represented from all sides of the party from Right, Left and centre to try and help the residents in Bartley green with there concerns.

During our campaign we have spoken to many residents about their concerns. I have arranged street stall. Spoke to MP’s, crime  commissioners but most importantly you the Voters. BG labour have produced leaflets, letters and used social media to send out daily updates of our progress. We also send a weekly newsletter to our members so they can share in our e

Having been born and raised in Birmingham, I have a huge amount of pride for this city, its people, and its achievements. To see it let down by a Labour-run council leaves me feeling angry and disappointed. I know that so many people are feeling the same, which is why I want to offer a hopeful alternative to years of neglect, decline, and indifference.

I am a chartered quantity surveyor responsible for managing the budgets of multi-million pound public projects, which gives me the experience needed to challenge and scrutinise the financial mismanagement which has left the city I love bankrupt and dependent on the approval of unelected commissioners who do not need to answer to the people their decisions impact.

Bringing back hope to a part of the city which has been neglected will require hard work, which is why I have spent the last eighteen months speaking to hundreds of people all across Billesley and helping to get things done, including tackling an illegal workshop, clearing flytipping, keeping people informed about planning applications that affect their lives, and campaigning to make our roads safe regardless of whether you drive or not.

Having worked with our two existing Green councillors, I have seen the difference it makes to have a Green in the room fighting for their local communities not vested interests, standing up for progressive ideals not hate and division, and standing in solidarity with the people who need it the most.

If elected, I will be that Green in the room for you, standing up for your community and working with you to shape our city.

The Green Party is the only major political party in the UK that is committed fully to a life based on democracy and justice within the planet's limits.

The Green Party has always dared to be different - and we’ve always known the power of good ideas. Birmingham has aspirations to tackle climate change, but candidates from the other parties lack the desire to implement the changes needed to transform this city.

Unlike others, we know that our planet has environmental limits, we understand that not everyone wants to live to work, that inequality is not just unfair, but damaging to everyone in society. And we have the bold solutions which allow us to offer inspiration in this age of uncertainty. 

Birmingham should lead the way, trialling Universal Basic Income, transforming existing housing stock into zero carbon homes and building homes and neighbourhoods for a very different future. There are many opportunities to be taken, but we need to start looking forward and understanding we can't go back to business as normal after the pandemic is over.

Jane Baston is standing as the Green Party’s candidate for Birmingham Selly Oak. She moved to Birmingham two years ago and has lived in the Selly Oak constituency since moving. She has been an active member of the Green Party for the past 4 years. Jane has worked closely with the local Green Party councillors to stand up for local services and will continue to do so as your MP.

Jane works for an educational charity and studied genetics and archaeology at University. She is an active UNISON member and is part of the Green Party Trade Union Group Committee, where she has campaigned locally and nationally to improve working conditions and to protect workers’ rights.

Jane has been the Co-Chair of the Green Party’s youth and student wing, the Young Greens, for the past three years supporting young people and students across England and Wales. She has a wide range of experience working with local groups and charities. She is passionate about campaigning for better funded youth services, safer streets, and fixing the housing crisis.

I was brought up in Birmingham and this city means everything to me. It has all the cultural wisdom and insight you could wish for but the Council has yet to embrace and harness it. We need to respect residents’ experience, knowledge and ideas about making the local area cleaner, safer, healthier, welcoming and more inspiring. By working respectfully alongside residents, we can help create places and environments where everyone thrives.  
Talking to the residents of Bournbrook and Selly Park is a thought-provoking and eye-opening experience. People tell you about things like flooding, bad landlords and road safety. They explain how their area has changed over the years and share their hopes and ideas for a better, healthier future.

Again and again, voters tell you that politicians have promised much but let them down. People are weary of vested interests and political squabbling. Birmingham is in trouble: residents have endured bin strikes, public service cuts, health-wrecking pollution and years of neglect. Solving these issues is urgent. We all pay our Council Tax and it isn’t too much to ask that all the basics work properly.

As a Green party member, I know that social and environmental justice go hand in hand. The poorest residents of the city suffer the most from pollution and speeding. Like many others, I’m desperate for this city to recycle our waste and stop burning our rubbish. As a cyclist, too, I share many residents’ desire for safe, healthy travel routes.

So the last thing we need is another generation of unimaginative and unresponsive politicians who forget to listen to the people they were elected to serve. Elected councillors must bring emotional intelligence and integrity to their roles and they have a responsibility to ensure that politicians work together for the common good. Council plans and policies must genuinely represent residents’ needs and wishes so that meaningful and sustainable improvements are delivered for everyone.

I’m always lea

Born in Tiverton Rd, I’ve lived in South Birmingham all my life, . That lifelong connection has given me a deep understanding of the community I would be proud to represent.

I was raised through the last 3 decades of the  last century Like many who remember those times, I look at Birmingham today and feel that it needs change

I want a better future—for my children, my grandchildren, and the local community. That’s why I’ve joined Reform, because I truly believe it’s the only party with the will to make real change happen.

I’m now semi-retired, but still driven to make a difference.Now, I’m stepping up for Bournbrook and selly park ward

Roxanne has worked in project management for a range of charities and non-profits, delivering projects that benefit communities. She has led environmental initiatives and volunteered with local organisations for years. Roxanne is committed to doing things with communities, not to them, ensuring residents’ voices are heard.

Roxanne is committed to protecting and investing in our parks and green spaces, so more young people and families can enjoy them. Drawing on her experience in project management for charities, Roxanne would work alongside local voluntary groups to help realise community-led plans to improve sports facilities at Rowheath Pavilion and develop a wellbeing centre at Claypits Pool.

Hello I’m Nicky Brennan, 36, and I live on Franklin Road with my children, who attend local primary and secondary schools. This community is our home, and I am committed to making it a better place for local families.

I previously served as the West Midlands’ first Victims’ Commissioner, where I worked to improve support and rights for victims of crime, particularly those affected by domestic abuse. That experience drives my commitment to protecting the most vulnerable and strengthening community safety.

As Cabinet Member for Housing, I am focused on tackling Birmingham’s housing crisis, including homelessness and the shortage of social housing. I am working to increase the number of safe, secure, genuinely affordable council homes, improve standards in temporary accommodation, and ensure people are not left without the support they need. I support renters’ rights because everyone deserves stability and dignity in where they live.

I am also a committed anti poverty campaigner. I believe every child deserves the best start in life, with opportunity, support, and the chance to fulfil their potential. I want to help build a city where aspiration is encouraged and no one is held back by their circumstances.

I believe in equality, fairness, and bringing people together. Birmingham is at its best when we are united as communities, not divided. I want to help move our city forward, creating opportunity, strengthening neighbourhoods, and ensuring everyone feels safe, respected, and supported.

Outside of work, I am a proud mum and an animal lover with three cats.

Richard was born in Birmingham, moved to Cotteridge with his husband ten years ago and enjoys walks in local parks with his puppy.  Professionally, he works in skills and education, helping people access training and employment. Richard cares deeply about the neighbourhood and wants to help ensure local services work well so that everyone has the chance to succeed.

In his job at a charity, Richard works with  employers to help young people gain skills for work. He will build stronger links between schools and local businesses and champion the supported internships scheme at Selly Oak Trust School. Richard would push for Council contracts and planning approvals to include jobs and skills opportunities for local young people.

Hiya! I'm Muhammad, I'm 26 and I'm standing to bring fresh energy and ideas into the council. I'm an Economic Analyst at the British Medical Association, and I previously worked in local government consulting. This has given me a real insight into the challenges facing our public services - as well as an understanding of how to fix them!

Birmingham needs serious leadership, with local representatives who understand the issues and are willing to work relentlessly to drive up standards and scrutinise service delivery. We need to be laser-focused on providing our core services and showing up for our communities - this means fixing potholes, tackling fly-tipping and solving Birmingham's housing crisis.

In addition to my day job, I'm a governor at my old school (King Edward's Camp Hill Boys), and sit on the National Members' Council of the Co-op. While at university, I was elected to the Students' Union for a year, representing 40,000 students and working closely with police and the city council to improve safety and community cohesion.

These roles have all given me really valuable experience in holding managers and executives to account, scrutinising policy and driving positive change - all of which are vital functions of an effective councillor.

I grew up in Birmingham while the Tories were underfunding our schools, closing youth centres and imposing austerity measures which cut our Council funding by over £1 BILLION. I want to help reverse this decline by creating more opportunities for young people, which will reduce antisocial behaviour and make our city the best place in the UK to grow up.

Fun fact: I've climbed Mount Kilimanjaro, trekked to Everest Base Camp and run 5 marathons for charity, raising over £10,000!

I am honoured to be standing for Reform in the Cotteridge & Bournville ward in the Local Election on 7th May 2026.

Having lived and worked in the area all my life, I have a deep understanding of the real issues that matter most to local residents. As a highly respected local business owner, I see first-hand the challenges facing families and small businesses, and I am committed to helping our community revive and thrive once again.

Through my experience chairing a business association within the ward, I know what it takes to bring people together and deliver positive change. I believe in practical solutions, strong local leadership, and putting residents first.

I care deeply about the future of Cotteridge & Bournville. I am standing because I want to make a real and lasting difference, ensuring our community is represented with integrity, common sense, and decisive action.

The Liberal Democrats are the only credible alternative in Bournville and Cotteridge for real change at the local elections on 7 May.

We’ve spoken to hundreds of people during the course of this campaign and the message is consistent: residents are frustrated that council tax keeps rising while the quality of local services continues to fall. People are concerned about the basics — such as the state of our roads, access to GP appointments, support for local schools & SEND, rubbish collection and local infrastructure.

Local politics should be about fixing these everyday issues. Our focus is not on distant high-level political arguments, but on practical improvements that make a difference locally.

If you want a council that listens and acts on the real concerns of our community, we are ready to provide that alternative.

NO to Kings Heath LTN street closures scheme

YES to traffic calming alternatives to the LTN;  YES to uniting a divided community and supporting the town centre;  YES to safer streets;   YES to reduced crime;   YES to protecting and enhancing the environment;  YES to a more accountable and responsive Birmingham City Council;   YES to better cycling and public transport provision;  YES to better equality and diversity including needs of disabled.

I have been a resident of hodge hill for over 25 years,

Hodge hill Bromford ward is the most deprived Area of Birmingham. 
Bin strike costing Birmingham millions of pounds. 
Our council tax on the rise .
Potholes on Roads and footpath. 
Roads flooded.
Rubbish problems 
Flytiping 
Criminal activities on the rise.
Street lighting poor.
Speeding motorists. 
Council properties in need of repair .
No youth centre for young children. 
Elderly people neglected. 
No road crossing at busy roads.
Labour councillors for this ward have let people down. 
We Liberal Democrats will make sure the funds available for this WARD are used wisely and appropriately .
We will bring in a system where councillors will be held to account for failing to deliver. 
Vote Liberal Democrats for a better future as you have seen the results of Labour Party.
I have been involved in many community projects on a voluntary basis.
I am taxi drivers representative also on voluntary basis. 
I would like to make sure this Area is not neglected again. 
I have been very vocal about the bin strike and have stood with bin workers to end the bin strike. 
I have also reported the flytiping and potholes around the Area.

Residents in hodge hill Bromford ward want to see a change for tye better and say we have seen the Area going downhill and poverty on the rise.
The 2 Labour Party Councillors are both cabinet members and have ruined this Area. 
Bin strike is as a result of these Councillors for Hodge hill Bromford ward. 
We need to make sure this Area is safe and peaceful for our residents. 
I will work with residents and will not be absent like 
I have first hand  experience of the problems facing residents.
Many candidates stand in local elections but  have no link with residents and just jump on the bandwagon during elections. 
Please check my social media platforms and you will see how I am involved in many community projects to make our Area's better. 
Make sure to vote for Liberal Democrats.

Worked in youth clubs.
Community projects 
School governor.

I have business in hodge hill .

I have been working with local people for many years.

I understand the problem faced by local residents.

We Liberal Democrats will ensure the funding available for Hodge hill Bromford ward is used sensibly and used in this WARD and not allow to goto another ward.

Labour councillors in this WARD have neglected this Area.

It is about time we take action against these uncompetant Labour Party Councillors who have ruined this Area.
Bin strike not ended and cost this city over 33.4 million pounds. 
IT systems so far cost has risen to over 120 million pounds. 
Roads parished .
Council properties sold.
The 2 councillors for Hodge hill Bromford ward are both cabinet members.

Both are Labour.

We Liberal Democrats will make sure residents do not suffer much longer.

Vote Liberal Democrats for a change .

As your candidate you will receive the following -

The potholes/roads dramatically improved ( quickly)
No labour waffle
Funding for community 
Dedicated email/WhatsApp for reporting issues and to reach out
Surgeries weekly 
To improve Castle Vale and for the whole of Birmingham to come together and be proud of our city 
A party which works locally for you 
Ex Military who does not shy away from a challenge

​My key priorities are to make Druids Heath feel safer, cleaner and greener.
​Everyone deserves to live in a place that is safe so you and your family can walk our streets with peace without fear.
​Our communities need to feel cleaner - showing that we still care and have pride for where we live, work and play

Deirdre Alden has been a Conservative Councillor for Edgbaston for 19 years. She lives in Edgbaston Ward, on the Calthorpe Estate.

Deirdre has helped many residents over the years and been involved in many campaigns – from opposing inappropriate planning applications, getting axed bus stops reinstated, going out on Streetwatch patrols with residents, to fighting for parking restrictions and CCTV cameras, and dealing day in and day out with residents’ problems such as missed bin collections, pot holes, and housing issues.

Deirdre works closely with Cllr Matt Bennett who is also standing in Edgbaston for the Conservatives. Together they have pledged to continue the work to protect Edgbaston’s heritage and Conservation areas. Under Labour, enforcement is insufficient and some Conservation areas in the city have been abolished because so many breaches of regulations rendered them meaningless. They will not let that happen in Edgbaston.

Deirdre and Matt will continue to press for more street cleaning at weekends as cleaning vehicles cannot access many Edgbaston streets in the week because of parked cars from the two Universities, QE Hospital and offices.

Air quality is a big issue in Birmingham and Conservatives have many policies to deal with it rather than introducing a crippling congestion charge. If Conservatives take control of Birmingham they will introduce green living walls in canyon style locations such as the Aston Expressway and by the Mail Box, introduce new technology such as “city trees” which have the cleaning impact of 275 trees but are the size of two benches, and increase the tree canopy in the city from the current 18% to 30% in line with other global cities.

Labour have introduced a policy of building on eight acres of parkland a year. Deirdre believes Birmingham’s parks are a valuable green lung which improve the health and quality of life for residents, and should not be built on.

Under Labour, the refuse system is a shambles. In E

I am so excited to be standing in the place I call home. 

This will be the first time I will be standing in the Local Elections - however if elected on May 6th. It will be a role that I will 100% be fully committed to.

Over the last 18 months I have been enjoying campaigning/working hard here in Moseley to make a difference in my home area. I love living here in Moseley and I love being out there in the community being very active and engaging with residents.

From volunteering litter picking (every Sunday morning) to helping set up a StreetWatch Team to speaking daily/weekly with local residents (completing a resident survey) to being part of the Community Residents Association Forum, as well as reporting on the issues that residents have raised to me, I feel I am ready to take it to the next level and represent you at Birmingham City Council.

Growing up in Erdington, I can see first-hand the impact of Tory austerity and Labour council cuts on our community. In standing on a platform of anti-cuts and anti-war, I will work with local residents, trade unions and public sector staff to fight for the services and funding we need.

Money can be found for bombs and dodgy PPE deals, but not for our streets or our youth. This is why we need someone in Westminster who will raise the voice of ordinary workers while also linking it to the need for a new mass worker’s party in the interests of the many.

Imran has lived in Hodge Hill for most of his life and attended Hodge Hill School. He has worked in education for over 12 years, represented parents as school governor, volunteered with the Youth Offending Service and also acted as a local football coach.

Having been a member of the Green Party for over six years, Imran stood as a local candidate previously. He is standing as a Parliamentary candidate now because he is passionate about social and environmental justice. He will campaign to highlight local issues which have been neglected, such as the poor standards of the local environment and the safety issues. Imran says:

“I will work to eliminate fly-tipping and keep our streets and parks clean, and make Birmingham’s roads safer by putting an end to speeding and knife-crime. Our libraries must stay open, and investment in our public services must continue. The cost-of-living crisis is affecting more and more of us. I want a fairer deal for us all.

“The ongoing crisis in the Middle East has highlighted the failure of our government to uphold international law and human rights. This must be addressed as it goes against our moral and ethical standards of upholding civil liberties and human rights for all.”

I'm standing for election in Handsworth because I'm fed up with the lack of action on the issues here.

Fly tipping and waste cover our streets. Our roads are covered in potholes. HMOs and crowded houses are becoming more and more common, with no thought of how it impacts our diverse community.

Handsworth has been left behind for years. I will fight for better transport, better facilities like reopening our swimming pool, longer opening hours for our library, more support for marginalised communities, action on speeding and antisocial behaviour and challenging the discriminatory Stop & Search policy.

I'm a mental health nurse by training, and I've dedicated my life to helping others, challenging the system and speaking up for people that get left behind.
I am standing as an Independent candidate for Handsworth because I believe our community deserves a councillor who listens, acts, and puts the community first.
Fresh Ambition & Unity for Birmingham

I’m a Harborne resident and have been an active safe streets campaigner in Harborne for several years. I have been involved in campaigns to reduce crossing times, tackle rat running through neighbourhoods and improve our street environment. You’ll find me on Friday mornings running a community kids bike ride to school. Or clearing the pathways where the council hasn’t had the capacity to. I want to see a Harborne with improved community services, better connectivity and thriving local businesses.

I love Harborne, and I can’t stand seeing what 14 years of Birmingham Labour has done to the place.

I’ve spoken to former Conservative councillors for Harborne who remember when, under Conservative leadership, Birmingham was voted the ‘cleanest city in Britain.’ Just look at how far we’ve fallen. People of my generation deserve to live in the same great city their parents did.

So here are our five ideas for Harborne:
• Put a stop to criminal activity in shops on our High Street
• Create a ‘Harborne Development Fund’ so local people can invest in local businesses
• Set up careers mentoring schemes for young people
• Protect the Harborne Festival
• Twin Harborne with an international suburb

That’s our local plan, alongside the wider Conservative six-point plan for Birmingham:
• Stop Labour’s plan for permanent fortnightly bin collections
• Fix the potholes now
• Crack down on slum landlords
• Protect our green spaces
• Tackle anti-social behaviour
• Fix Labour’s bankruptcy

Support the Conservative plan with your vote. Beat Labour and say no to Reform!

Thank you!

I was born in Birmingham and raised in Edgbaston, and my experiences at Queen Elizabeth Hospital Birmingham inspired me to pursue a career in medicine. At medical school, I led one of the largest volunteering efforts during the pandemic, supporting hospitals, GP practices, hospices and NHS staff across the region. I was honored to be awarded a British Empire Medal (BEM) in HM The Queen’s Birthday Honours for services to healthcare. I am currently working as an A&E junior doctor in the West Midlands. I’m standing because public service has always been central to who I am - I care deeply about helping people and want to serve our community as a strong, accessible voice.
One of the two Harborne Independents candidates alongside Martin Brooks. Putting people before party and working for Harborne all year round.

As a proud Birmingham resident, I am committed to serving the people of Heartlands with real action. I will tackle the potholes damaging our roads, Flytipping, Bin collections, action illegal HMOs, enforcement officers against pavement parking, residential only parking permits, Crime stopper, Women safety, installation of speed cameras to keep our streets safe. Vote for me and Reform UK, we can transform Heartlands.

As a lifelong Brummie, I'm committed to Birmingham Green Party's manifesto for bringing hope back to our city. Birmingham has so much to be proud of, from our rich cultural heritage to our calm green spaces and canal paths. Yet residents are bearing the brunt of poor decision making, that fails to make the most of all the things that make Brum such a great place to live. Instead we're left with housing inequality and rubbish piling up on our streets.

The Green Party does things differently to the two-party political system that many people are tired of. If elected, my focus will be on working with residents rather than taking a top down approach to making decisions. Our manifesto is built on listening to what people are telling us on the doorsteps, and developing policies that respond to their concerns. Pushing for an end to the bin strikes so we can all live in a clean environment, large-scale council house building to tackle homelessness and the housing crisis, and protecting support for children with SEND are just some of our priorities. We're campaigning for safer streets and better public transport too. But we can only start working to implement our manifesto by having Green councillors elected.  If I'm elected on May 7th, I'll start working immediately alongside residents to push for our manifesto priorities to be implemented. It's time to make Birmingham a city where everyone feels safe, valued, and hopeful for our future.

I was born and raised in Birmingham, and I experienced first-hand what living under a Conservative government looked like, as our country and its greatest institutions slowly crumbled around us. Now I fear the rise of Reform and the similar damage they will do to my city and my country. I want to fight for a better Birmingham, where we can grow through common understanding and hard work, not by punching down at each other or magic solutions.

I am standing in Highter's Heath to offer a fresh voice to our community - one that will not stop fighting for us all and isn't afraid to say when times will be hard, but we can get through them together.

I make three pledges as a candidate:

1. **An active visible Councillor** - You won’t hear from me once every four years; I will tirelessly work for Highter's Heath and always keep you involved and informed on my work!

2. **A representative not an individual** - I will consult the residents of Highter's Heath on making decisions that are right for you, not impose them on you!

3. **Unity not division** - I will aim to bring the people of Highter's Heath together through the politics of community, not conflict. We can come together to stop a Reform administration that will cut our council’s most vital services and damage the city we love!

Birmingham born, having spent over 29 years living in Holyhead, I’ve dedicated my life to working within & for the community. Supporting youth, building creative platforms, and creating real opportunities through creative projects, Artz & Vybz and The Melanation.

With over 15 years of experience in the events, communications & human resources industry and avid student of migration & anthropology studies.
I don’t just understand the challenges here, I live them daily. As a full-time carer, parent & active member of the area, I see first-hand the pressures facing families across Holyhead.

I’m running for Councillor to restore trust, raise standards, and ensure our community is properly supported, represented, and able to thrive—not just survive.

My commitments:
Resolve the Bin & Waste Crisis
Urgent resolution of bin disputes through negotiation with a revitalised proactive restructure.
Ensure reliable waste collection services.
Address fly-tipping by improving infrastructure.

Challenge Council Waste & Overspending
Advocate for reviewing senior salaries, council spending & Opposing wasteful contracts.
Push for transparent budgeting & responsible taxpayer use.

Improve Community Safety
Campaign for better street lighting & public space safety.
Address parking & visibility issues for pedestrians.
Focus on practical crime prevention measures, addressing poverty & living standards.

Support Young People & Families
Expand youth programs & mentoring.
Advocate for better support services for families & local opportunities for youth beyond school.

Raise Living Standards
Support initiatives to reduce poverty drivers.
Push for housing & environmental improvements.
Advocating for policies enhancing residents' quality of life.

Champion Local Enterprise & Opportunity
Support entrepreneurship, career development & create local partnerships for investment. Promoting skills linked to economic opportunities.

Be a Visible, Accountable Representative
Present in community, regular

Rob Grant is a Birmingham City Councillor representing Kings Norton South, which is within the Northfield constituency. As a councillor, he knows the many deprived areas in Northfield and the uneven level of investment since the closure of MG Rover in 2005, which has left many areas behind. If elected to parliament Rob will apply his broad life and professional experience to stand up for local communities, organisations, and businesses. Rob is passionate about issues that affect our quality of life: housing, transport, health, and pollution. He will campaign for proper funding for the NHS and social care, and effective action on pollution and the environment. Rob says: "I have lived in our diverse and energetic city for 32 years. I have worked in education for the past 20 years, currently as a college lecturer, and I have first-hand experience of the concerns of young people. "If elected I will work tirelessly to achieve a vision of an improved social, economic, and greener Northfield. I will represent the interests and aspirations of local communities and businesses. I will challenge existing approaches to tackling and resolving issues and concerns from central and local government, and champion a drive towards decentralisation and for local decision making."

“I was born and raised in the West Midlands, and I am proud to have made Ladywood my home. I love being part of such a vibrant, diverse community, and I want to help make it better.

“I have worked in homeless services in our area and seen firsthand the harm done by Tory cuts and Labour’s poor governance. I know we need to work hard to improve our public services. I am ready to do that work.

"I have signed the Safer streets for Birmingham campaign pledge, and both the Brummies united against racism and Palestinian solidarity candidate pledges. Equality is at the heart of my politics and I will always stand up for marginalised people here in Ladywood and abroad.

"In Ladywood I will campaign for cleaner safer streets, improved public services, better regulation to prevent rogue landlords from abusing tenants, and will campaign from day one to get around the negotiating table to end the Birmingham bin strikes.

"I feel the Ladywood Regeneration has been handled abysmally, and I will campaign from day one for the consultation to restart with residents at the heart of planning and transparency in all aspects of the project.

“We need a bolder politics. Labour have spent years ignoring Ladywood and failing our whole city. It’s time for change. Vote Green!”

We’re proud to call Birmingham home. Ladywood is one of the most vibrant and diverse communities in our city, but we also know that too many residents feel unheard, especially when it comes to regeneration, housing, and the everyday issues that affect our streets.

After the Conservatives took £1bn from our city, we know things have been tough. But with a Labour government backing councils, we’ve secured over £650m in new funding to protect and rebuild our vital services.

We’ll always put Brummies and Ladywood first - helping residents with the cost of living and backing our young people.

Labour are introducing breakfast clubs, expanding free school meals and taking 60,000 children in Birmingham out of poverty. And your Labour council is putting £1m into youth services.

We’ve been out over the past year – not just at election time – listening to residents, standing up for our community, and pushing for fair solutions shaped with local people.

Based on these conversations, we’ve developed some key priorities:

We’ll push for fair, transparent regeneration that protects Ladywood’s identity

We'll tackle poorly managed HMOs and Secure Exempt Accommodation with action against rogue landlords

We'll Improve the bin service and clean up our streets – tackling fly‑tipping with increased enforcement

And we’ll invest in our community – reopening the neighbourhood office, supporting local businesses, improving safety for women and girls, and making sure growth works for local people.

Labour will bring unity to our city and challenge division wherever it appears. There are others in this election trying to divide us, pit neighbours against each other and talk Birmingham down.

We know Birmingham is strongest when we stand together.

We care deeply about Ladywood, and we’re determined to work with residents, listen, and represent you.

We’d be honoured to have your support.

Sally and Satvir

After the Conservatives took £1bn from our city, we know things have been tough. But with a Labour government backing councils, we’ve secured over £650m in new funding to protect and rebuild our vital services.

Sally and Satvir.

I’m the Ladywood’s Voice for Accessibility and Integrity
I was born and raised in Ladywood, and having lived here for nearly 39 years, I have spent my life navigating the streets and services of this ward. I am not just a candidate; I am a disabled accessibility advocate with almost four decades of lived experience. I understand the barriers our community faces because I face them every day.
Why I am standing:
I believe that accessibility is not a "future consideration"—it is a fundamental right. I am running to ensure that every resident of Ladywood, regardless of their circumstances, has a voice that is heard and respected.
My Commitments to You:
Common-Sense Accessibility: I will fight for inclusive public spaces, ensuring our buildings and streets are accessible to everyone, from wheelchair users to parents with prams.
Honest Representation: I am a candidate of integrity who refuses to be silenced by rigid rules or party branding that ignores local needs.
Advocacy for the Vulnerable: My background in community spirit means I will always prioritise supporting our most vulnerable citizens over political games.
Ladywood is my home. On 7 May, vote for a candidate who has spent 38 years in this community and will never stop fighting for it. 🗳️ Vote Jade Marie Cotton

My name is Abhinav Priyadarshi, and I am standing as your candidate for Reform UK because I believe our communities deserve honest leadership, practical solutions, and a clear vision for the future.
I come from a background in engineering and technology, where results matter more than promises. I am stepping forward not as a career politician, but as someone who understands real-world challenges — from the rising cost of living to the need for better opportunities, stronger communities, and efficient public services.
My priorities are simple and focused:
Economic growth and opportunity
Supporting innovation, jobs, and businesses so that everyone has a fair chance to succeed.
Cost of living relief
Pushing for practical policies that reduce everyday financial pressure on families.
Efficient public services
Making sure your taxes are used wisely, with accountability and transparency.
Stronger local communities
Investing in safety, infrastructure, and opportunities that improve quality of life.
Future-focused leadership
Bringing modern thinking, technology, and long-term planning into decision-making.
I believe politics should be about serving people, not systems. Too often, voices go unheard — I am here to change that.
This is not about empty promises. It is about delivering measurable impact, standing firm on principles, and representing you with integrity.
If you want change, accountability, and a forward-looking approach — I ask for your support.
Together, we can build a stronger, more prosperous future.
Abhinav Priyadarshi
Candidate, Reform UK

"Hi, I’m Abigail Smith, I'm excited to announce that I am standing for Northfield Ward in the by-election on July 4th. Following the resignation of the Labour councillor, I am determined to bring positive change and strong representation to our community. Here are some of the key issues I will focus on:

Protecting Our Heritage and Parks: I am committed to preserving our local heritage, such as the Kings George V pub, and safeguarding our green spaces like Manor Farm Park from unwanted development.

Tackling Crime, Drugs, and Gangs: I will work closely with the police to tackle crime, gangs, and antisocial behaviour in our area, ensuring a safer environment for all residents.

Campaign to Keep It Weekly: I will campaign to maintain weekly bin collections and keep our area clean and tidy, so everyone can have pride in where they live.

Together, we can make Northfield a better place to live. Your support is crucial, and I look forward to meeting many of you over the coming weeks. 

Vote for me on July 4th for a brighter future for Northfield."

I am passionate about Birmingham and proud to campaign and fight elections in my home of Ladywood. Residents often tell me that they are tired of a complacent Labour taking their votes for granted – and I relish giving them more choice in elections.

I have long campaigned for Mental Health service provision to receive full equality with physical health – a journey started by the LibDems in government, and one we must continue. Social Care is in crisis and carers urgently need fair wages and better support. All of this means that we have a big mountain to climb, but I have been lucky to meet some inspirational people across Birmingham who are working to make this happen, and I continue to support them.

I’m a qualified Teacher and so very much aware of the pressures faced by teachers and staff, as well as students. I support young people in various ways, such as helping them grow their campaigning experience and supporting them standing for election.

I also believe it’s vital that our environmental protections are strengthened, and that civil liberties for LGBT+, BME and others are protected and improved.

I’m standing to be a strong voice for our community. I’ve seen firsthand where the system is failing people and I’m determined to change that.
I will stand up for residents, demand better services, and fight for a fairer deal for everyone!

Key Details of His Candidacy:
Active Community Role: Costa highlights that he is already assisting residents through a local WhatsApp group.

Campaign Priorities: His platform focuses on issues like crime (specifically linked to HMOs, and exempt propertied), parking, and improving the physical appearance of the area, with links to business and youth investment.

Independent Stance: He explicitly advocates for independent leadership over party-driven politics and has been vocal about his campaign on social media platforms like Instagram and Facebook.

Official Confirmation: He officially announced his candidacy in March 2026, positioning himself as a candidate who "cares about the area".

I’m standing because Newtown deserves better.
Safer streets, cleaner neighbourhoods, and services that actually work — not excuses and delays.
I will listen, show up, and act. Not just during elections, but all year round.
No politics. No noise. Just real work for local people.
If you want change, I’m asking for your support.

With the largest mandate ever in the UK, over three years ago, 17.4 million people voted for us to leave the E.U. 

Here in Northfield over 60% voted to leave but your decision continues to be ignored

​

Voting for the same career politicians will only give the same result again. It is time for change. We need politicians with experience of the real world and a vision to see this through. 

Northfield deserves better - the whole Country deserves better.

​

I live and work in Birmingham and have 

Established and run a successful small business in Birmingham

Headed a long established Birmingham charity

Educated myÂ children in Birmingham

Campaigned and worked for good inter-faith relations

Put myself forward to represent Northfield for Parliament, the City Council and as West Midlands Police and Crime Commissioner.

 

I am ready to be your voice where it matters

I grew up in North Edgbaston, and I know first-hand what it’s like to live in a community that doesn’t always have the support networks young people need to thrive. For many families along Dudley Road and City Road, life can feel like a constant struggle to find opportunities, stability, and a path forward.

I was fortunate to get my first job at 18 on Dudley Road, working at Lidl while supporting myself through education. I grew up on welfare. I understand the fight, the uncertainty, and the determination it takes to keep going when the system isn’t built for you. And I know that none of us should have to do it alone.

I’ve worked hard to find my footing in this world and now it’s time for me to give back to the community that shaped me. That’s why I’m standing in this election. I want every young person, every family, every resident to feel the support, opportunities, and investment our area deserves. We are a community full of talent, strength, and resilience. With the right backing, we can achieve so much more.

I’m running because I understand the struggle. I’m running because I believe in this community. And I’m running because it’s time we lift people up, not leave them behind.

I am 24 years old and work as a plumber, running a small business serving customers across Birmingham, my working life is grounded in the real economy. Working in people’s homes, I speak to ordinary residents every day and see first-hand the challenges families face with local services and the condition of our city.

My interest in politics began in my early teens, when I started to reject the prevailing left-wing worldview that had been presented to me for much of my life.

Even at that age, I felt strongly that it was traditional values that hold our society together family, community, faith, and national identity and that these were increasingly being undermined in the name of “progress.”

Around that same time, I also found my Christian faith, something I hope continues to shape my understanding of responsibility and service to others.

This election feels different because the political landscape itself is changing.

We are witnessing the growth of sectarian politics and the "unholy" coalition on the left.

I feel it is essential that residents are offered a credible alternative, one rooted in British values, civic responsibility, and respect for our nation.

That’s why I’m standing for Reform UK.

We are the only alternative. Reform is the only party willing to challenge the political consensus that has dominated British politics for too long.

I am excited to be part of this once in a lifetime political revolution, and if elected will work tirelessly to deliver for residents on that basis.

I’m Esther Rai, your Labour candidate for the Northfield ward by-election on Thursday 4 July 2024.

I’ve spent my working career as a teacher and in community support work, advocating for children, young people, and the most vulnerable.

As your councillor I will stand up for your interests, be present in the community, and champion Northfield.

Let’s get Northfield back on track.

My pledges to you: 

1. Be present within our community - I will get the basics right - I’ll hold regular ward forums and advice surgeries and get our ward plan over the line.

2. Fight for every penny we’re entitled to - I’ll make sure all of our ward funds are spent on improving Northfield, and that we get a fair share of new pots of money.

3. Work towards a better future for our high street - I’ll work with the BID and Council to make sure we’re ready to win future funding and to stop anti-social parking from food couriers.

4. Stand up for you during the Council’s recovery - I’ll be your voice in the room to make sure decisions are made with the people of Northfield, including saving Northfield Library.

5. Make our streets safe and accessible - I’ll work with authorities to improve walking, wheeling and bus routes, and with the PCC to reduce crime on our streets.

Birmingham is at a crossroads after 14 years of Labour misrule which resulted in huge council tax rises, devastation of public services and finally - bankruptcy. Only the Local Conservatives have the experienced leadership to start to put things right on Day One after the election and have a costed and detailed six point plan to put the city back on the right road. This plan includes fixing the roads quickly to prevent more expensive repairs later, ending the bin strike and continuing weekly collections, protecting the city’s green spaces, cleaning up the streets and dealing with badly run HMOs and balancing the budget. Only voting for the Conservatives can guarantee that we will not be landed with another desolate 4 years of Labour control.

I have had my home in the Northfield area all my life. It really is home to me. I know the people and what’s happening here. If elected I will of course stand up for Northfield and carry out a weekly ward round to keep me in touch with the people and the area in which we live. If something’s not right I will do my best to get it put right. And I will support the Conservative administration to help it set the city back on its feet.

Birmingham needs a Conservative administration to undo all the damage done by Labour and Northfield can do its part by voting for me on 7 May to ensure a Local Conservative councillor is playing his part to help get all the necessary work done as soon as possible.

Why I am standing: I strive to honour my ancestors sacrifice. My ancestors did not fight and die in WWI & II for the state that the United Kingdom is in at this very moment. I am living proof that being British is not about skin, it is about your values.

Trust: The public’s trust in our political class is completely gone, and rightfully so. I do not want power over anyone - only over myself - and I want the same for others As long as you are not hurting anyone you should be able to live your life how you see fit; with freedom of speech and freedom of religion - I will put my life on the line for these things.

Cost of living: First, tackle inflation and bad government by bringing back the gold standard: money printing, fiat currency and fractional reserve banking must go! We must never allow a Central Bank Digital Currency to be foisted upon us. If we go back to money that the establishment cannot control - then we, the people, can prevent corruption and unjust wars. It would be a stepping stone to the greatest peace of our time if we went back to sound money.

About me: I am a 25 year old libertarian who attended John Willmott and Bishop Vesey. I have a degree in economics with finance (graduated 2020) from Queen's University Belfast. I have worked many jobs including: financial crime (PwC), labourer, gas tool decontamination worker and postman.

Unlike all too many of our current politicians... I did NOT grow up with a silver spoon in my mouth.

I live in the constituency in which I am standing; my family have lived in the constituency for decades.

I enjoy new challenges and fitness.

I’m standing for the Green Party because it has values that I share: caring for people, caring for the environment, and promoting local voices and local action. I worked in the NHS for 20 years as a psychologist and am acutely aware of the damage the pandemic has done to the mental health of children and adults. I know levels of loneliness in our society have increased and poverty is on the rise. And these are also bad for our mental health. I want us to work together to combat poor mental health on a community level.

Access to green spaces and connections with other people are beneficial for wellbeing so I want to see these protected and enhanced in our patch. I’m already a Tree-Keeper for Perry Common Meadows Tiny Forest. I want to see the people of Perry Common, Short Heath and the New Oscott estate develop our own local plan in partnership with the council to protect the quality of life of us all.

My name is Ahmed Ghulam, and I am standing as an Independent candidate for Perry Common Ward because I believe our community deserves a stronger, more practical, and more accountable local voice.

I have lived and worked in Birmingham for many years and have built my professional career around solving real problems, managing projects responsibly, and working directly with residents and communities. I am not affiliated with any political party — I am standing independently so that I can represent you, not party interests.

My priorities are simple, realistic, and focused on what matters most to local people.

My commitments to you:
	•	To listen to residents and act on their concerns
	•	To be visible, accessible, and accountable throughout the year — not just at election time
	•	To work constructively with the council, the police, and community groups to improve daily life in Perry Common
	•	To always be honest about what the council can and cannot do, and how public money is being spent

My key priorities for Perry Common:
	1.	Safer streets and neighbourhoods
	•	Work with West Midlands Police and local safety teams to improve visible policing and response times
	•	Tackle anti-social behaviour, fly-tipping, and nuisance activity that affects residents’ quality of life
	•	Support better lighting, CCTV where appropriate, and safer public spaces
	2.	Clean, well-maintained neighbourhoods
	•	Push for regular street cleaning, quicker waste collection responses, and stronger action against fly-tipping
	•	Ensure parks, pavements, and public spaces are properly maintained
	•	Hold contractors and the council accountable for poor-quality work
	3.	Fair use of public money
	•	Challenge wasteful spending and demand transparency in council decisions
	•	Support investment that delivers real value for residents, not just paperwork and consultation
	•	Ensure local services are funded where they make the biggest difference
	4.	Support for families, older residents, and the vulnerable
	•

The Cost of Living Crisis continues to impact the area of Pype Hayes. As the Green Party candidate and resident of Pype Hayes for 14 years, as well as fulfilling my councillor duty to serve everyone in the ward, I also intend to work hard to:

- Eliminate Pot Holes Across Pype Hayes (all candidates say this, but it is important).
- Improve cross-community relations (e.g. Pype Hayes needs much better community hubs, we don't have anything that compares with Erdington nor Sutton Coldfield).
- The Cost of Living crisis will hit us hard again over 2026 thanks to the war on Iran, we can tackle that through resource-sharing (FairShare, Food sharing, Car sharing).
- Make Pype Hayes Clean again (programmes to reduce litter and dumping, but also car idling at schools, which is illegal anyway).
- Reduce the Cost of Living through low-carbon community projects: Solar PV, EVs, Cycling through community projects.

I am a born and bred Brummie: I grew up here, graduated from Aston University and stayed here after Uni.
I am passionate about the values that the Green Party stands for. We deserve to live in a fairer, better society, where wealth does not accumulate in the hands of the already rich. We are the sixth richest country on the planet and yet we have police officers and nurses being forced to resort to foodbanks and an increasing number of homeless people on the streets - not to mention issues like zero hour contracts that put all risk and uncertainty onto the workers and allow companies to maximise revenue.
We stand for a fairer society all round: we would insist on companies paying their fair share of tax and use that to help the worst off.
And we can not continue to prioritise the economy above all other considerations: we live on a finite planet and the idea t hat infinite economic growth is not just feasible but required is absolute madness. Sooner or later we will hit hard planetary boundaries on what the ecosystem can regenerate each year: in fact we already use more than the planet can sustain - and one thing is for sure when the human created laws of economics conflict with the immutable laws of nature there can be only one winner.

I am standing in Quinton because I want to bring a practical, hard-working approach to local representation.

Public service, responsibility and listening to people properly are values I care deeply about. My priorities are cleaner streets, safer neighbourhoods, better local services and real accountability from the council. I want to be a visible and approachable voice for Quinton residents.

I'm currently a student at the University of Gloucestershire, studying Ecology and Environmental Science. It's clear to me with every day that the situation is urgent and needs immediate attention. As a scientist, I simply can't sit on my hands and ignore it. 

My uni which was recently voted the most sustainable in the UK, and I'm proud to be a part of an institution that prioritises climate action. I'm aware thought that I'm also living in a town that declared a Climate Emergency without taking meaningful steps to address it.

Although I'm in the middle of my studies, I know the climate crisis isn't going to wait until I'm done. And it's because of this that I want to represent my fellow students, and fight to protect their interests. I've been campaigning for rent and tuition reductions to reflect the quality of our courses through the pandemic, as well as advocating for LGBTQIA+ protections.

I'm frustrated that sitting politicians aren't doing enough, and I want to stand for environmental issues in my home ward as well as pressing issues such as underemployment, active travel connectivity, and quality of local educational institutes. Last year I was elected as a co-covenor of the Green Students Committee, which has been absolutely brilliant!

When not studying, I've been volunteering with gardening in Winston Churchill Memorial Gardens, which has been very rewarding. I really miss doing Parkrun 5k at Pittville Park, and with my interest in Aston Villa and the Beers, Wine and Spirits Society, I'm the candidate you can certainly have a drink with once lockdown's over!

Shuranjeet Singh was born, raised and currently lives in Handsworth. With a background in political science and health policy, he is involved primarily in community mental health work and local volunteering. ☺️

He leads a local mental wellbeing service, is a school governor and leads a local neighbourhood forum in his spare time. 💪🏽

Shuranjeet wants his local community to meet their amazing potential - and focuses on challenging fly tipping, improving local housing, tackling antisocial behaviour, and supporting our parks and green spaces. 🌹

You can read more about Shuranjeet at:

www.shuranjeet.com

Nigel has lived in Birmingham for the last 30 years and for the last 20 of these has lived on St Paul’s Square in the Jewellery Quarter. Nigel is a local businessman who has operated a number of well known city pubs over the time ha has lived here. Two of his passionate hates are litter and graffiti. Having recently become a pensioner Nigel is also concerned about the state of pavements, lack of seating on the streets and total lack of public toilets. Another concern for older people is the uncontrolled use of e scooters and e bikes on the pavement.

I joined the Liberal Democrats in 2017.  Previously I had taken liberal values for granted;  I had seen the movement though much of the 20th century for votes for women, decriminalising homosexuality, movements towards greater equality and progress against discriminatory pracices in the workplace and many more.

In recent years it has become more and more apparent that these Liberal values should be actively celebrated.  Against a backdrop of nationalism and chauvainism and faced with a world that was moving away from the trend towards tollerance and inclusivity I realised that if we wanted to maintain the progress we have made and if we want to avoid falling back to a more shameful era in our history we must step forward and campaign for what we believe in.

We face unprecedented challenges in this time: environmental issues - including but not limited to climate change, poverty - and the pernicious effects that poverty brings, intollerance - ant the exclusionary effects this can have on our society.  Some of these things can only be tackled at the naional level, some internationally but many of these issues can be addressed a the local level with the right policies based on strong evidence and sound judgement.  I hope I can count on your support to take a small step to making our lives better.

Jerry Evans is a professional archaeologist writing reports on Roman pottery for a living. He was a City Councillor representing Springfield Ward, one of four in the Constituency, from 2003 – 2016. During his time as Councillor, and before, he wrote over 34,000 letters on behalf of constituents to the Council and other authorities. Every year he was a Councillor Jerry wrote over 2,000 casework letters on behalf of local people!

On the Council Jerry fought for effective regeneration monies for our area. He also worked hard to clean up our area. He chaired the Hall Green Constituency Committee from 2006 – 2011. He is contesting Hall Green against Labour Brexiteer Roger Godsiff. The majority of people in Hall Green voted Remain and Jerry will be the only mainstream party candidate to represent that view.

He said “I intend to be a diligent constituency MP if I am elected, standing up for the people of Hall Green. I will oppose Tory plans for a hard Brexit and benefit cuts. I undertake that I will continue to live in the Hall Green constituency, unlike our current MP.”

Three years ago I took early retirement after a working life in engineering. Plans to restore Stirchley Baths as a community hub were by then well advanced and I joined the volunteer programme even before I had set foot in the building. I have loved being a part of it and am also active in several other local community groups, all completely non-political. That sense of community has been enhanced by new businesses that have come to the area, joining successful ones that have been here for many years. There’s a lot of energy and creativity making a positive difference to Stirchley right now.

At the same time, the area has suffered from the unwanted effects of would-be developers, as the demolition site at the end of my road reminds me daily. We face uncertainty about the future usage of currently vacant land, and the ever-present problem of empty commercial properties, especially along the Pershore Road corridor.

Add to that the problems that afflict residential areas and the city as a whole – especially the dreadful state of the street cleaning services – and it’s clear that the newly elected council, and the councillor for Stirchley, will have a full agenda.<

As a resident here for 28 years, and a member of the Liberal Democrats for even longer than that, I’m pleased that the party’s proposals for Birmingham are so relevant to the needs of the area. One of our top priorities is to clean up the city: to reverse Labour’s cuts to the refuse collection service and to clamp down hard on flytipping. Our plans to give local communities real control over spending will allow each area to set its own priorities and to build the kind of partnerships that will be essential to the regeneration of Stirchley.

Another key concern of local residents – the constant spread of HMO’s (houses of multiple occupation) – will be addressed by extending selective licensing of landlords, eventually to the whole of Birmingham. I’m especially concerned to ensure that planning regul

Hi there:)  with the elections only a month away, I'd like to introduce myself as the Green candidate for Four Oaks ward on both Birmingham City Council and Sutton Town Council. 
I grew up here. My kids both have gone to Arthur Terry, just like I did. I love this area: the safe streets, the sense of community, and the park on a sunny afternoon. Four Oaks is a genuinely great place to live, and I want to help keep it that way.
But, like many I'm also frustrated by many things that are happening. I'm frustrated by the lack of support for the basics ... the ongoing bin strikes  and the general state of our roads and streets. By the general mismanagement that's meant a decline in services you take for granted. Families like mine have had children missing school due to strikes and I know how hard it is to get a GP appointment.
I'm not here to make noise or pick fights, but be a positive voice for change that benefits everyone. I am someone who'll roll up their sleeves and actually get things done.
To those who are put off by the Greens policies or personalities nationally, I'd like to just ask them to think about what matters at a local level. The Greens are all about investing in local services and working together to make our communities better and safer.  I believe this is our main difference to other parties. 
If you live in Four Oaks and want a safe pair of hands representing you, I'd love your support.

Promoted by Leigh Alex Graham (Green Party) at 2A, Blackthorn House, Birmingham B31RL

I am on my third term of office as Councillor Resident in my own ward, married, three children, school governor and deeply interested and involved in local community.

I believe in protecting our heritage, hence my involvement in local park groups and nature policy group for Birmingham.

It is important that we retain the character of our local environment in Sutton Coldfield, and the other Vibrant Urban Villages in Birmingham.

I want to see progress on Urban Design Study for Sutton Coldfield which will lead to other much needed improvements.

Ben Auton has lived in Sutton Coldfield for 6 years and loves the town for its vibrancy and radically caring community. Ben is an environmental scientist working in remediation having worked to clean up Birmingham’s canals and currently works removing pollution from former gas works.

His interests are focussed on helping people solve housing issues and directly combatting the poor state of rental housing. He works with Unite and Acorn community unions to bring about change through solidarity and direct action.

“I am very policy oriented and am standing to become the Green MP for Sutton Coldfield because our policies are the only ones which take our critical climate condition seriously. At the same time as tackling the existential climate emergency we have to reform our society to be fairer. There is no climate justice without social justice.

“Green values are about taking responsibility for our community and country. We need real hope and real change.”

I’m Joe Blenkinsop and I’m standing to be your local Town and City Councillor for Sutton Trinity ward.

As a local resident with a young family I’m determined to ensure a thriving future for our town and City.

Please vote for me to deliver the change our town and city so desperately need.

Midlands-born Jenny is a committed campaigner and activist, determined to make a difference in our community. She says, "We are living through the most challenging times. People across our towns and villages need a strong voice to stand up for them and represent their interests. When I speak with residents across the constituency, I hear their concerns about the cost-of-living crisis, difficulties accessing NHS services, and the large amount of new development.

Outside of politics, Jenny is a mum to three boys and has been actively involved in the community as a charity trustee, Scouts group chair, and school governor. Professionally, she is an accountant and business consultant. She has worked with a wide range of private and public sector entities over her 30-year career, and a significant part of her work has been focused on tackling fraud and corruption. Honesty, integrity and fairness are core values for Jenny.

Jenny is passionate about education and campaigns for increases in school funding. She is concerned about environmental issues, pushing for greater protection of our natural resources as well as urgent action to tackle the climate emergency. She believes in the importance of encouraging local residents, particularly young people, to be engaged in and involved in their communities.

Jenny says, "For me, being involved in politics is all about helping people make the changes that are needed to improve their lives. I'm devastated by the damage being done to our country by the current government, so I'm fighting for the Liberal Democrat values of freedom, fairness and equality, working with individuals, community groups and businesses to build a brighter future for us all."

Zoe is standing to be a voice for Ladywood in Westminster: to advocate for decent homes, education, healthcare, and transport, to speak out about the challenges Ladywood faces, to protect its resources like the reservoir, the residents of the Ladywood Estate and more. Zoe is a leader who will take action to protect our planet and your lives, as well as your children’s futures.

Zoe believes that the Green Party’s policies on Universal Basic Income, public transport, free childcare, inclusivity and protecting the planet, are all key to building that better future. Zoe says:

“Growing up in Ladywood, I know first hand that despite being statistically one of the most deprived areas in the UK, Ladywood is in fact rich. Rich in diversity, talent, history, grassroots activity, sports, the arts, education, and much, much more! Ladywood deserves an advocate who will celebrate all it has to offer, while fighting for better, much better, provision for residents.

“The Green Party envisions a better, fairer society. I want to be part of creating that society for Ladywood – it is perfectly possible if enough of us act together. Will you join me?”

13 years of Labour mismanagement has made Birmingham unrecognisable. It is time for the people to take back control with robust and honest representation, securing a return to cleaner and safer streets, a return for enterprise and business and secure hosing and social services.

Having grown up in Hay Mills and lived her for two decades, I am deeply invested in making our streets cleaner and safer for the community. I will fight to end the bin strikes, protect our vital public services, contribute to city-wide policies that affect the ward, and work to bring the fresh investment the city deserves.

I’m a lifelong Ward End resident, a father of three, and someone who understands our community because I live here. I went to local schools here, my children now attend those same schools, and I’m proud to serve as a school governor.

I’m not a career politician - I’m standing as an independent to put residents first.

For too long, Ward End has been neglected, with poor roads, rising fly-tipping, and local issues ignored while costs go up.

I’m standing to deliver real change - cleaner streets, a stronger community voice, safer neighbourhoods, better services, and more opportunities for local people.

Ward End deserves better - and together we can make it happen.

I was born and raised in Birmingham, with 20 years in property and four school-age children. I’ll deliver better housing, safer streets, stronger services, and real opportunities locally for everyone.

My name is George Webb, a hardworking campaigner from Weoley and a proud Brummie through and through.

I was born at the old Selly Oak Hospital, attended Bournville Junior and Secondary School, and later graduated from Newman University. It doesn’t get much more local than that.

I currently work as a Level 4 support staff member at a special needs school here in Birmingham, a role I’ve proudly held for the past three years. Before that, I worked as an educational mentor, supporting some of the most vulnerable and challenged young people in our city. That experience has shaped who I am and strengthened my commitment to making a real difference.

So why am I standing for election?

Because I love this city, and I am tired of seeing it in the headlines for all the wrong reasons. Birmingham is full of potential, but it has been neglected for far too long. Our roads are crumbling, our bins are going uncollected, and too many people feel let down. Enough is enough.

We need change, we need to fix Birmingham, and I am ready to be part of that change.

I have only ever wanted to stand in Weoley and Selly Oak. This is my home, it is where my family lives, where my friends are, and where I care deeply about the future. To represent our community in the council chamber, and to continue making a positive difference locally, would be a real privilege.

I am a university academic working in **clean‑tech and AI**, and a proud mum of three. I am passionate about creating low‑cost, autonomous, energy‑generating homes powered by renewables, and driving green skills programmes in education that prepare our children and young people for high‑quality, future‑proof jobs. Community safety is paramount to me, and I will be a powerful, unwavering voice for women, children and family-safety across our ward.

Here is what I will fight for in Weoley & Selly Oak:

- **Lowering the cost of energy** by championing renewables and green energy solutions across our ward, helping families cut their bills while creating local jobs in the clean energy sector.

- **Stronger community safety** by ensuring a named, contactable police officer is assigned to every part of Weoley & Selly Oak, with faster response times and visible neighbourhood policing. Our ward will have a named, contactable police officer in every community, with a guaranteed 72‑hour response, tripling Community Safety Officers and introducing new Respect Orders to ban persistent offenders.

- **Green upskilling for our next generation** through real education changes that prepare young people in our schools and youth centres for the jobs of the future.

- **Cleaner streets and faster action on fly‑tipping** by deploying simple digital reporting tools so residents can instantly report problems, combined with tougher enforcement, more regular street cleaning, increased CCTV use and new powers to seize offenders’ vehicles.

- **Investment in every corner of Weoley & Selly Oak** by repairing potholes and pavements, backing local projects through Thriving Neighbourhoods, and restoring pride in our green spaces and parks.

I stand with **Stewart Holroyd** in Weoley & Selly Oak. Together, we will build a cleaner, greener, safer and more inclusive Weoley & Selly Oak.

I’m **Stewart Holroyd**, and I am standing to be your local Labour councillor for Weoley and Selly Oak.

I work as a caseworker, helping residents deal with real-life council issues, from **planning applications**, to securing the right **SEND support**, to resolving long‑running **housing problems**.

I am standing because I want to put my knowledge and experience to work for our community, and help people where I live.

My priorities for Weoley and Selly Oak are:

- **Ending the bin strike** by pushing for a fair and lasting resolution. It has gone on for far too long. Residents are fed up, and they deserve a reliable service.

- **Cleaning up fly‑tipping** so our streets and green spaces are safe, tidy, and somewhere people can take pride in.

- **Fixing pothole‑ridden roads** to make our streets safer for drivers, cyclists, and pedestrians alike.

I am standing alongside **Dr Kiran Gulia**. Together, we are a strong local team focused on getting things done.

Baber Baz is a hard working candidate that is going to stand in the local election in Yardley West & Stechford on May 3rd 2018. Baber works all year around campaigning and listening to the voice of the residents in the area. Comparing to fellow cadidates who are up against Baber, residents have mentioned that he is the only candidate that is door knocking; handing out leaflets/flyers and has been recognised greeting and meeting people in Yardley and Stechford. Residents have also mentioned that currrent/previous councillors in Yardley & Stechford only turn up at election time, whereas Baber (candidate) is in the area all year round. Let this election on May 3rd be a fantastic opportunity for a true candidate, that has the willingness and thrive to improve the area and tackle problems as well as be there for others. Please support Baber by Voting for Him and help him become a councillor Yardley West & Stechford.
